supply_chain.dependencies: Principal technology and related services (including credit card transaction processing, production and related services) are handled via contractual arrangement with Fiserv. Fiserv is the sole source or one of a limited number of sources of the services they provide.
supply_chain.risks: Failure of third parties to provide various services that are important to operations could have material adverse effect. Difficult to replace Fiserv in timely or seamless manner. Principal agreement with Fiserv expires December 2030.

regulatory_risks: CFPB final rule on credit card late fees - lowers safe harbor from $30 to $8, would result in significant reduction in interest and fees on loan receivables
Dodd-Frank Act restrictions on business practices, stringent capital, liquidity and leverage requirements
Basel III capital requirements - may increase regulatory capital requirements and limit dividends/stock repurchases
Enhanced prudential standards for savings and loan holding companies with $100+ billion assets - biennial supervisory stress tests, formal capital plan submission, stress capital buffer
CFPB authority to prevent unfair, deceptive or abusive acts or practices
Federal Reserve Board and OCC restrictions on dividends and stock repurchases
FDIC restrictions on brokered deposits if not well-capitalized
Privacy, information security and data protection laws (GLBA, CCPA, CPRA, GDPR)
Anti-money laundering and anti-terrorism financing laws (Bank Secrecy Act, Patriot Act)
OFAC sanctions programs compliance
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements
Military Lending Act (MLA) protections for active duty military
CARD Act restrictions on interest rate increases and late fees
Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) requirements
State sales tax rules and regulations
Section 1033 of Dodd-Frank Act final rule on open banking data sharing
Proposed Basel Endgame capital rule changes for banks with $100+ billion assets
Proposed long-term debt requirements for holding companies with $100+ billion assets
State attorneys general enforcement actions
Interchange fee regulations and restrictions

sanction_risks: OFAC sanctions compliance requirements for transactions with sanctioned countries, terrorists, narcotics traffickers, and weapons proliferation entities
